facts.title: A divided peninsula
facts.fact: Since 1948 the peninsula has been divided into the Republic of Korea (South) and the DPRK (North), separated by the DMZ near the 38th parallel.
facts.korean_en: Korea is divided into south and north.
facts.note: South Korea's official name is **대한민국**; the DMZ truce line is the 휴전선.

facts.title: A young, lively democracy
facts.fact: South Korea is a democratic republic with a directly elected president. Its democracy is young — direct presidential elections returned only in 1987 — and turnout is famously high.
facts.note: 투표를 하다 = "to vote" (literally "to do a vote"). Many nouns pair with **하다** to form verbs.
